title: REPLACE Display Name
source_url: REPLACE: URL to official rules PDF or publisher rules page

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content: REQUIRED. Full component list with exact counts. Example: '19 terrain hexes (4 forest, 4 pasture, 4 fields, 3 hills, 3 mountains, 1 desert), 6 sea frame pieces, 18 number tokens...'

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content: OPTIONAL — omit this subtopic entirely for card-only or tile-laying games with no central board. For games with a board: step-by-step instructions to assemble the play area.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content: REQUIRED. What each player receives and does before the first turn. Include: pieces, cards, starting resources, player board setup.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content: REQUIRED. How to determine first player, any initial placement or drafting phase, opening hand or resources. Everything between 'setup is done' and 'the first turn begins.'

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content: REQUIRED. What happens on each turn (or round), in order. Number the phases. Example: '1. Roll dice → 2. Collect resources → 3. Trade → 4. Build.'

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content: REQUIRED. All actions a player can take on their turn. Include costs, restrictions, and outcomes. Be specific — this is where players will look up 'can I do X?'

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content: OPTIONAL — omit for simple games. Cover game-specific systems: robber, traitor mechanics, hidden roles, drafting, area control, combat, etc. Add ADDITIONAL optional subtopics below this one for games that need them (e.g., a separate 'Trading' or 'Combat' subtopic).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content: REQUIRED. What triggers the end of the game, how final scoring works, ALL victory point sources, and how ties are broken.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content: REQUIRED. What new players should focus on in the first few turns. Specific, actionable advice — not 'have a plan.'

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content: REQUIRED. 3–5 specific mistakes new players make and why they're mistakes. Example: 'Don't hoard cards past 7 — you'll lose half when a 7 is rolled.'

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content: OPTIONAL — omit for very simple party games. The 2–3 biggest strategic trade-offs in the game. Example: 'Cities vs. expansion — cities double income but cost rare resources.'

metadata.created_by: REPLACE: halfling | elf | dwarf | human | goblin
